tasks.title: abortedStarts Set grows unboundedly — no eviction for completed sessions

tasks.title: disposeActiveDecoder() is called with void in failSession/postBudgetExceeded without awaiting, so a subsequent onCancel that also calls disposeActiveDecoder may enter a second dispose race
tasks.category: unawaited promise / fire-and-forget on error path
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'unawaited promise / fire-and-forget on error path' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: EncodeHandler constructor fires run() with .catch() but the catch calls failSession which itself calls disposeActiveEncoder with void — if encoder.dispose() throws, the rejection is silently swallowed
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'task leak / unawaited promise' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: run()'s Promise.all([feedDecoder, readDecoderEvents]) — if readDecoderEvents throws, feedDecoder is abandoned without cancellation, keeping the decoder's async iterator alive indefinitely
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'resource leak / task leak' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: run()'s Promise.all([feedEncoder, readEncoderChunks]) has the same abandoned-task hazard as decode-handler — if readEncoderChunks throws, feedEncoder continues pushing pixels to an encoder being disposed
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'resource leak / task leak' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: No AbortSignal or cancellation token threads through feedDecoder/readDecoderEvents — cancellation relies on polling this.ended after every await boundary
tasks.reason_for_tier: finder hint 'complex'

tasks.title: handleReleaseState cancels handlers with void decode.onCancel() but does not await, so decodeSessions.delete runs before onCancel's disposeActiveDecoder resolves
tasks.defer_reason: The void+delete pattern is intentional for hard teardown. The onSessionEnd callback problem is separately identified as concurrency-13. The question of whether the old handler's async tail can corrupt state after release_state+new-start depends entirely on concurrency-13's epoch-guard gap. Concurrency-12 alone describes expected behavior; the real bug is concurrency-13.

tasks.title: onSessionEnd callback decodeSessions.delete has no epoch guard — a stale handler finishing after release_state+new-start will delete the new handler's map entry
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'resource leak / callback-after-teardown' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: onSessionEnd callback encodeSessions.delete has the same no-epoch-guard race as concurrency-13 for encode sessions
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'resource leak / callback-after-teardown' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: No test covers the release_state + immediate new decode_start race that triggers the onSessionEnd stale-delete bug (concurrency-13/14)
tasks.category: missing test coverage for concurrency scenarios

tasks.title: Exception thrown inside run() finally block can suppress the original error
tasks.reason_for_tier: category 'swallowed exception / leaking finally' defaults to sonnet (standard local work)

tasks.title: Output size guard uses 1 byte/pixel minimum but does not account for multi-byte formats before allocating

tasks.title: run() rejection caught by a lambda that calls failSession, but failSession itself can throw if callbacks.onSessionEnd throws
